passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q. Nos. 12-16) In the questions given below, some relationship have been expressed through symbols as show below. Study the meaning of these symbols and pickup the correct answer from the answer choices for each of the questions below.

⌀ means 'less than'
∆ means 'not greater than'
~ means 'equal to'.
+ means 'not equal to'
× means 'not less than'
= means 'greater than'.

Directions (Q. Nos. 17-22) In the following questions, the symbols @, ©, $, % and # are used with the following meaning as illustrated below.

'P $ Q' means 'P is not greater than Q.'
'P @ Q' means 'P is neither smaller than nor equal to Q'.
'P # Q' means 'P is not smaller than Q.'
'P © Q' means 'P is neither greater than nor equal to Q.'
'P % Q' means 'P is neither smaller than nor greater than Q'

Now, in each of the following questions, assuming the given statements to be true, find which of the three Conclusions I, II and III given below them is/are definitely true and give your answer accordingly?

Directions (Q. Nos. 23-27) In the following questions, the symbols @, ©, $, % and # are used with the following meaning as illustrated below.

'A $ B' means 'A is not smaller than B'.
'A # B' means 'A is not greater than B'.
'A @ B' means 'A is neither smaller than nor equal to B'.
'A © B' means 'A is neither smaller than nor greater than B.'
'A % B' means 'A is neither greater than nor equal to B'.

Now, in each of the following questions, assuming the given statements to be true, find which of the three Conclusions I, II and III given below them is/are definitely true and give your answer accordingly?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: X = Y = Z does not implies

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements D # K, K @ T, T $ M, M % J
Conclusions I. J @ T    II. J % T    III. D @ T

choices.c: Either I or II is true
choices.d: Either I or II and III are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements R @ N, N © D, D $ J, J # B
Conclusions I. R @ J    II. J @ N    III. B @ D

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements W © B, B % V, V $ R, R @ K
Conclusions I. K © B    II. R # B    III. V @ W

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements H $ M, M # T, T @ D, D © R
Conclusions I. D © M    II. R @ M    III. H $ T

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements B %, J @ K, K © T, T $ F
Conclusions I. F @ K    II. B @ K    III. B @ F

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements F # B, B $ M, M @ K, K © N
Conclusions I. N @ M    II. F $ M    III. K © B
